IsA: It is a game using an "Impulse Area" system.
It is a game using an "Area Impulse" rules system.
HasA: It has a 16" x 44" mounted map.
It has a simplified game system.
It has a short introductory scenario.
It has a long campaign scenario.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by street-to-street fighting simulation.
It is characterized by an "Impulse Area" system.
It is characterized by a simplified ruleset requiring only ten pages.
It is characterized by a map divided into 74 different areas.
It is characterized by a point-to-point movement system.
UsedFor: It is used for simulating street-to-street fighting during the Battle of Stalingrad.
It is used for controlling German forces trying to take Stalingrad.
It is used for controlling Soviet defenders.
It is used for teaching the rules through an introductory scenario.
It is used for playing a long campaign that can be divided into several short scenarios.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by Don Greenwood.
PublishedIn: It was published by Avalon Hill.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Avalon Hill.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a map of a portion of Stalingrad.
It consists of almost 400 double-sided counters.
It consists of a short introductory scenario.
It consists of a long campaign scenario.
PartOf: It is part of a series of games using an "Impulse Area" system.
It is part of a series of games using an "Area Impulse" rules system.
KnownFor: It is known for its "Impulse Area" system.
It is known for its simplified rules.
It is known for its map divided into 74 areas instead of hexes.
It is known for its point-to-point movement system.
It is known for simulating the Battle of Stalingrad.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Battle of Stalingrad.
It is associated with Avalon Hill.
It is associated with Don Greenwood.
It is associated with the "Impulse Area" system.
It is associated with the "Area Impulse" rules system.
NamedAfter: It is named after the Battle of Stalingrad.
About: It is about street-to-street fighting during the Battle of Stalingrad.
It is about the Battle of Stalingrad.
It is about controlling German forces trying to take Stalingrad.
It is about controlling Soviet defenders.
It is about the strategies of the Eastern Front.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Storm Over Arnhem.
It was influenced by the "Area Impulse" rules system.
It was influenced by the need for simplified rules.
